reasoning: The NFL owners meeting is scheduled for May 20-21, 2025, which falls just before the June 1 resolution date. At the prior meeting in early April, a proposal to ban the "tush push" was tabled after facing opposition from 16 teams, leaving it short of the 24 votes needed to pass. However, a revised and more expansive rule prohibiting the pushing or pulling of any ball carrier anywhere on the field has been drafted. Commissioner Roger Goodell believes a consensus will be reached, and highly reliable NFL insiders, including Mark Maske (Washington Post) and Adam Schefter (ESPN), have recently reported (May 4-6) that the revised proposal has strong support and is "expected to be banned" or has a "reasonably good chance" of passing. The strong consensus among insiders and the structural change to the proposal to capture more votes strongly points toward the rule passing, though the 24-vote supermajority threshold leaves a non-negligible chance of it falling short.
